admission_type: free access with verification of curricular requirements and assessment of personal readiness (admission test, with grade-based exemption)

academic_requirements.summary: Free-access master's degree (120 CFU, 2 years) requiring a Bachelor degree plus verification of curricular requirements and an assessment of personal readiness. High-performing graduates of related degrees are exempt from the assessment test.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A Bachelor's degree of at least 180 CFU/ECTS, or an equivalent qualification recognised as suitable.
academic_requirements.curricular_credits: At least 36 CFU (or equivalent knowledge) in the scientific-disciplinary fields (SSD) for basic training of the Information Engineering (L-8) or Computer Science and Technology (L-31) degree classes, and at least 42 CFU (or equivalent knowledge) in the SSD indicated for the characterising training activities of those classes.
academic_requirements.automatic_qualification: Graduates of specified UniGe bachelor programmes (including Electronic Engineering, Computer Engineering, Biomedical Engineering, Informatics and Management Engineering) are automatically considered to meet the curricular requirements.
academic_requirements.grade_threshold_for_exemption: Candidates are exempt from the admission/readiness test if their Bachelor's degree final grade is at least 9/10 of the maximum, or corresponds to ECTS classification 'A' or 'B'.
academic_requirements.three_curricula: Three curricula are offered: Artificial Intelligence and Human Centered Computing; Software Platforms and Cybersecurity; Sustainable Systems Engineering.
academic_requirements.international_qualification: Non-EU candidates residing abroad with a foreign qualification apply through UniGeApply for pre-evaluation of curricular requirements before enrolment.

accepted_proofs: A recognised English-language certificate at level B2 or higher (among those accepted by the University, listed on the CLAT page https://clat.unige.it/en/CertificazioniRiconosciute).
A Bachelor's or Master's degree taught in English.
A B2-level English exam passed during the undergraduate degree.
Candidates lacking certification may sit a free B2 English test through the University's CLAT language centre.
exemption_rules: Holding a recognised B2-or-higher English certificate, an English-taught degree, or a B2 exam passed during the bachelor satisfies the English requirement.
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: English proficiency is verified during the admission procedure / assessment of personal readiness; international applicants verify it during pre-evaluation on UniGeApply.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: Assessment of personal readiness (no TOLC; a document-based evaluation by the admission Commission). NOT a TOLC-I.
entry_exam_or_test.mandatory: Mandatory only for candidates who do not meet the grade-based exemption.
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: Applicants to the Computer Engineering master who do not qualify for the automatic grade exemption.
entry_exam_or_test.format: The Commission scores submitted documentation across criteria: academic potential (max 50 points), relevance of the bachelor's degree (max 30 points), quality of the awarding university (max 20 points), and other CV aspects (max 10 points).
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: A score of 85 or above results in admission.
entry_exam_or_test.exemption: Graduates with a final bachelor grade of at least 9/10 of the maximum (or ECTS 'A'/'B') in related engineering/computing degrees are exempt from the test.
entry_exam_or_test.interview: No interview is described; the assessment is document-based.

rounds: Spring session for EU citizens and non-EU residents in Italy (UniGeApply): opens 9 April 2026; English-taught master's degrees close 28 August 2026.
Open-access master pre-registration on the UniGe calendar: from 9 April to 30 November 2026, and until 10 March 2027 with late payment.
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: Confirmation of enrolment (first year): from 15 July 2026 to 30 November 2026, and until 10 March 2027 with late payment. Enrolment in years after the first: from 15 July to 5 October 2026, with arrears accepted from 6 October to 5 November 2026.
application_deadline_eu.notes: The programme-level deadlines page does not distinguish EU vs non-EU first-year application windows; EU/resident applicants use the spring UniGeApply session and the standard enrolment calendar.

rounds: Fall session for non-EU candidates requiring a visa (UniGeApply): opens 26 November 2025; institution-wide close 20 March 2026; pre-evaluation results by 22 May 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: After UniGeApply eligibility, visa applicants complete the Universitaly pre-enrolment and apply for a study visa.
application_deadline_non_eu.early_closure: [uncertain] No programme-specific early closure is listed for the Computer Engineering master; the only published fall early closures are Medical-Pharmaceutical Biotechnology (13 Feb 2026), Maritime Science and Technology bachelor (18 Feb 2026) and Computer Engineering bachelor (8 Mar 2026) — the bachelor, not this master.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Non-EU visa applicants must use the fall session via UniGeApply; the A.Y. 2026/27 fall window has now closed (closed 20 March 2026).

required_documents.notes: Uploaded on UniGeApply (max 2 MB per document).
If still in progress, the degree must be completed by 15 August 2026.
Used to verify the curricular CFU/SSD requirements.

required_documents.document_name: English language proficiency certificate (B2 or higher) or equivalent proof
required_documents.notes: Recognised B2 certificate, English-taught degree, or B2 exam passed during the bachelor; otherwise a free CLAT B2 test.

required_documents.document_name: Valid passport (non-EU) and visa/pre-enrolment documents via Universitaly
required_documents.notes: Non-EU visa applicants complete Universitaly pre-enrolment after UniGeApply eligibility, then apply for the study visa; foreign qualifications require Declaration of Value or CIMEA Statement of Comparability.

source_quotes.quote: COMPUTER ENGINEERING, Master's Degree (D.M. 1649/2023), Class LM-32, Teaching language English, Genova, 120 CFU, 2 years.
at least 36 CFU, or equivalent knowledge, in the scientific-disciplinary fields (SSD) ... at least 42 CFU, or equivalent knowledge, in the SSD indicated for the training activities characterizing
If you obtain a score of 85 or above, you will be admitted.
a grade of 9/10 or higher of the maximum grade required ... are exempt from the test.
Spring session opens 9 April 2026; Master's degrees close 28 August 2026.
Fall session (non-EU visa applicants) opens 26 November 2025, closes 20 March 2026; results by 22 May 2026.

uncertainty_notes: This is the MASTER degree (LM-32, code 11965), distinct from the Computer Engineering bachelor (code 12133, L-8 R, Imperia). Kept separate.
No programme-specific fall early-closure date is published for this master; the only listed early closures are for two bachelors and the Medical-Pharmaceutical Biotechnology master.

The programme-level deadlines page lists the generic UniGe enrolment calendar and does not separate EU vs non-EU first-year application windows; EU/spring and non-EU/fall windows are taken from the institution-wide UniGeApply pages.
